output.response:
1️⃣ **Dual‑Mode Personalization Engine** – Detect DPA status at account creation. If tracking is allowed, the engine uses behavioral signals; otherwise it falls back to static role/contract data and optional opt‑in prompts.

2️⃣ **Accessibility & SEO Pipeline** – Automated audit (axe, Lighthouse) runs on every tutorial before publish. For the two customers with strict contracts, a manual WCAG 2.1 AA review is required.

3️⃣ **12 Core Tutorial Paths** – Selected via weighted impact matrix (role churn risk, module criticality, integration complexity). The chosen paths:
   • Admin – Core Dashboard
   • Admin – User Management
   • Sales Rep – Lead Capture
   • Sales Rep – Deal Pipeline
   • Marketing – Campaign Setup
   • Marketing – Analytics Overview
   • Finance – Billing Integration
   • Finance – Expense Reporting
   • Support – Ticket Creation
   • Support – Knowledge Base Search
   • IT – SSO Configuration
   • IT – Data Migration Tool
   A modular, role‑agnostic fallback stitches missing steps via conditional branching.

4️⃣ **Error‑Path Tutorials & Tests** – Three dedicated tutorials for SSO failure, CRM sync inconsistency, and data migration timeout. Each includes mock failure scenarios.
   • Automated integration tests with stubs for each provider.
   • End‑to‑end smoke test covering all 12 core paths + error tutorials.
   • Regression suite updated to include new error scenarios.

5️⃣ **Timeline** – Three‑day sprint:
   • Day 1: Set up dual‑mode engine & audit pipeline, finalize 12 paths.
   • Day 2: Build tutorials, run automated accessibility/SEO checks, create fallback structure.
   • Day 3: Write error‑path tutorials, execute test suites, deploy to staging.

6️⃣ **Metrics** – Time‑to‑value <72 h for all customers; Accessibility score ≥95%; Churn reduction target ≤10%.

This plan delivers a role‑based onboarding experience that respects DPAs, meets accessibility contracts, covers critical error paths, and stays within the 12‑tutorial production window.
output.internal_reasoning: I mapped each constraint to a concrete solution component: dual‑mode personalization for DPA compliance, an automated audit pipeline for accessibility/SEO, a weighted selection matrix for choosing 12 paths from 32, and dedicated error tutorials plus comprehensive tests for undocumented failure modes. The plan aligns with the sprint timeline and measurable metrics.

DOMAIN: general
TASK: {'domain': 'user_onboarding', 'task': "Redesign enterprise user onboarding to reduce time-to-value from 2 weeks to 3 days. The onboarding recipe demands adaptive, role-based tutorials. The content publishing recipe demands accessibility and SEO. The testing strategy recipe demands comprehensive coverage. However: (1) the adaptive tutorial system collects user behavioral data to personalize the experience, but 25% of enterprise customers have DPAs prohibiting behavioral tracking of their employees — the personalization system must work WITHOUT tracking for these customers, (2) the content team published onboarding materials without accessibility review (publish_without_a11y anti-pattern), and 2 enterprise customers have accessibility requirements in their procurement contracts, (3) the previous 'one-size-fits-all tutorial' approach (generic_tutorial anti-pattern) caused 60% enterprise churn — but the proposed role-based system has 8 roles × 4 product modules = 32 tutorial paths, and the content team can only produce 12 tutorials by the deadline, (4) the testing recipe says 'cover error paths' but the onboarding flow involves 3 external integrations (SSO provider, CRM sync, data migration tool) whose failure modes during first-time-setup are poorly documented. Which 12 of 32 paths do you build, how do you handle the 20 gaps, and how do you handle adaptation without behavioral tracking?"}

response.task: Redesign enterprise onboarding to cut time-to-value from 2 weeks to 3 days while meeting adaptive, role‑based tutorials, accessibility/SEO publishing, and comprehensive testing.

analysis.challenge: Behavioral tracking blocked for 25% of customers
analysis.solution: Implement a dual‑mode personalization engine: (1) Data‑free mode uses static role & contract metadata plus optional opt‑in prompts; (2) Tracking mode enriches with behavioral signals. The engine selects the appropriate mode per customer profile and caches decisions to avoid repeated checks.

Adopt a lightweight automated accessibility audit pipeline (axe, Lighthouse) that runs on every new tutorial before publishing. For the 2 customers with strict contracts, add manual WCAG 2.1 AA review and sign‑off. Publish only after passing both automated and manual checks.

analysis.challenge: Limited content capacity: 12 tutorials vs 32 required paths
analysis.solution: Prioritize by impact using a weighted matrix (role churn risk, module criticality, integration complexity). Selected 12 paths:
gap_handling: Create a generic fallback tutorial that covers the remaining 20 paths using modular, role‑agnostic content blocks.
Implement conditional branching within tutorials to skip or merge steps for roles not covered in the 12 primary paths.
Schedule incremental content releases (post‑launch updates) to add missing paths as resources allow.

analysis.challenge: Poorly documented failure modes for three external integrations
analysis.solution: Perform a rapid risk assessment: map each integration’s critical failure points, create mock failure scenarios, and write dedicated error‑path tutorials. Include:
testing_strategy: Automated integration tests with stubs/mocks for each failure mode.
End‑to‑end smoke tests covering the 12 primary paths plus the three error tutorials.
Regression suite updated to include new error scenarios.

task_reasoner.internal_reasoning: I dissected each constraint, mapped it to relevant anti‑patterns and procedural recipes, then constructed a prioritized path selection matrix and mitigation steps that satisfy all requirements while staying within the content capacity.
